data.title: Pinterest's Sales Forecast Misses Wall Street Expectations
data.description: Pinterest has announced its sales forecast for the current quarter, which did not meet Wall Street's expectations. Bloomberg posted on X that this comes shortly after the company revealed plans for layoffs and a strategic shift towards artificial intelligence products. The social media platform's recent moves indicate a significant transition in its business strategy as it adapts to changing market demands. Despite these efforts, the projected sales figures suggest challenges in meeting investor expectations.

data.title: Fiserv Introduces INDX for Real-Time Crypto Settlements
data.description: Fiserv, a prominent payments company, has unveiled INDX, a real-time cash payment system designed specifically for cryptocurrency firms. According to NS3.AI, this platform facilitates instant U.S. dollar settlements through a single custodial account and is operational 24/7. Additionally, INDX offers up to $25 million in insurance coverage, backed by FDIC protection.

data.title: Applied Materials Projects Strong Sales Amid AI and Memory Chip Demand
data.description: Applied Materials, the leading U.S. supplier of chipmaking equipment, has issued a positive sales forecast for the current quarter. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the company anticipates increased demand driven by artificial intelligence and memory semiconductors. This optimistic outlook suggests a robust market for chipmaking equipment as industries continue to invest in advanced technologies. The company's forecast reflects the growing importance of AI and memory chips in various sectors, contributing to a surge in equipment purchases. Applied Materials' projection underscores the ongoing expansion in the semiconductor industry, fueled by technological advancements and increased demand for high-performance computing solutions.

data.title: Airbnb Reports Strong Fourth-Quarter Bookings and Positive Revenue Outlook
data.description: Airbnb has reported robust bookings for the fourth quarter, attributing the success to strong travel demand and the increasing popularity of its new flexible payment and booking options. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's optimistic revenue forecast. The positive outlook reflects Airbnb's strategic efforts to adapt to evolving consumer preferences in the travel industry.

data.title: Oracle Secures $88 Million Cloud Contract with U.S. Air Force, Boosting Stock by 16%
data.description: Oracle has successfully obtained an $88 million cloud contract with the U.S. Air Force, strengthening its position in the Department of Defense's cloud modernization efforts. According to NS3.AI, this development has led to a 16% increase in Oracle's stock value this week, prompting Wall Street analysts to raise their price targets for the company. Despite differing opinions, several firms consider Oracle a promising investment in the software industry, with some price targets reaching as high as $400.

data.title: Westpac Reports First-Quarter Profit Increase Driven by Home Loans and Institutional Lending
data.description: Westpac Banking Corp. has reported a rise in its first-quarter profit, attributed to an increase in home loans and robust growth in institutional lending. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's performance in these key areas. The Australian bank's financial results reflect a positive trend in its lending activities, contributing to its overall profitability. This development comes as Westpac continues to focus on expanding its lending portfolio, particularly in the housing and institutional sectors. The bank's strategic emphasis on these areas has played a significant role in boosting its financial outcomes for the quarter.

data.title: Mixed Results for Major European Stock Markets
data.description: Major European stock markets closed with mixed results. According to RTHK, Germany's DAX index ended at 24,852 points, experiencing a slight decline of 3 points. Meanwhile, France's CAC index rose by 27 points to close at 8,340 points, marking an increase of 0.3%. In contrast, the UK's FTSE 100 index fell by 69 points, closing at 10,402 points, with a decrease of nearly 0.7%.

data.title: Rivian Anticipates Greater Losses Amid Cost Management Efforts
data.description: Rivian has cautioned that it might experience a larger-than-anticipated loss this year as it strives to manage expenses before the launch of its next-generation SUV. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by the electric vehicle manufacturer in balancing cost control with its ambitious production goals. The company is focusing on streamlining operations to mitigate financial setbacks while preparing for the introduction of its new SUV model. Rivian's efforts to optimize its financial performance come at a crucial time as it seeks to strengthen its position in the competitive EV market.

data.title: Gallup Ends Longstanding Tradition of Tracking Presidential Approval Ratings
data.description: Gallup, a well-known American polling organization, announced on Wednesday, the 11th, that it will cease tracking presidential approval ratings, ending a tradition that has lasted for 88 years. According to Ming Pao, this decision follows a recent social media post by U.S. President Donald Trump, in which he criticized polling organizations, suggesting that 'false and misleading polls should be considered a criminal offense.'

data.title: FTC Warns Apple Over Alleged Suppression of Conservative Views on Apple News
data.description: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) issued a warning letter to Apple CEO Tim Cook on Wednesday, accusing the company's news platform, Apple News, of potentially suppressing conservative viewpoints, which may violate consumer protection laws. According to Ming Pao, FTC Chair Ferguson referenced a report by the conservative watchdog organization, Media Research Center (MRC), which highlighted that none of the 620 popular news stories pushed by Apple News in January originated from right-wing media outlets such as Fox News or the Daily Mail. Ferguson emphasized that if Apple is filtering content based on political bias, it could mislead consumers. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) Chair Carl also expressed support for the FTC's stance, criticizing Apple for allegedly suppressing conservative perspectives.

data.title: SoftBank Group's Profit Boosted by OpenAI Investment Valuation Growth
data.description:
SoftBank Group, a major player in technology investments, has reported a profit of 248.6 billion yen for the third fiscal quarter ending in December. According to Ming Pao, this marks a significant turnaround from losses in the previous year and represents the fourth consecutive quarter of profitability for the group, a feat not achieved since 2021. However, the profit was only about 30% of analysts' expectations.

The valuation growth of OpenAI has been a key driver of SoftBank's financial performance. By the end of last year, this investment contributed a paper profit of $19.8 billion to SoftBank, effectively offsetting fluctuations in other business areas.

data.title: Hong Kong Stock Market Sees Decline Amid Tech Weakness
data.description:
The Hang Seng Index fell by 233 points, closing at 27,032 points, with a trading volume of 238.7 billion HKD. According to Ming Pao, the Hong Kong stock market continues to fluctuate within a range, with technology stocks showing persistent weakness. Tencent (0700) experienced a downward breakout, although a theoretical rebound window has opened.

AI-related stocks remain a hot topic, with MiniMax (0100) and Zhihu (2513) seeing significant increases of 14.6% and 28.7%, respectively. Some lagging AI application stocks also showed movement, with Insilico Medicine (3696) being a potential buy on dips.

data.title: SEC Discusses Key Initiatives Before Senate Banking Committee
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) engaged in discussions with the Senate Banking Committee, focusing on its ongoing initiatives and regulatory efforts. U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) posted on X, highlighting the importance of the dialogue in shaping financial regulations.

The meeting aimed to address various aspects of the SEC's work, including its role in overseeing financial markets and ensuring investor protection. The discussions are part of the SEC's broader strategy to enhance transparency and accountability within the financial sector.

The committee meeting provided a platform for the SEC to outline its priorities and challenges, emphasizing the need for effective regulation in a rapidly evolving financial landscape. The SEC's engagement with the Senate Banking Committee underscores its commitment to maintaining robust oversight and adapting to new market dynamics.

data.title: Polymarket Introduces Rapid Bitcoin Price Prediction Market
data.description: Polymarket, a decentralized prediction platform, has unveiled a new market for predicting Bitcoin prices. According to NS3.AI, this market updates every five minutes, allowing traders to make short-term predictions. The initiative is designed to offer a dynamic tool for monitoring Bitcoin price expectations in near real-time.

data.title: Allies Commit $35 Billion in Military Aid to Ukraine for Air Defense
data.description: UK Defense Secretary John Healey announced that allies have committed up to $35 billion in new military aid to Ukraine. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this aid aims to bolster Ukraine's air defenses following significant Russian air strikes targeting energy infrastructure and civilian areas. The support underscores the international community's response to the escalating conflict and its impact on Ukraine's security and infrastructure.

data.title: SEC Chairman Highlights Regulatory Focus on Prediction Markets
data.description: U.S. SEC Chairman Paul Atkins informed the Senate Banking Committee that prediction markets are a key regulatory focus. According to NS3.AI, Atkins highlighted the jurisdictional overlap between the SEC and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), noting that primary oversight tends to lean towards the CFTC. The SEC and CFTC coordinate closely through weekly meetings to address these regulatory challenges.

data.title: Dollar Index Rises Amid Currency Exchange Rate Fluctuations
data.description: The dollar index, which measures the U.S. dollar against six major currencies, increased by 0.1% to close at 96.928. According to Jin10, by the end of the New York foreign exchange market, the euro was trading at 1.1866 dollars, down from 1.1882 dollars the previous day. The British pound was at 1.3623 dollars, compared to 1.3637 dollars the day before. The U.S. dollar rose against the Japanese yen, trading at 152.94 yen, up from 152.83 yen. It also traded at 0.7697 Swiss francs, slightly lower than the previous day's 0.7703 Swiss francs. Additionally, the dollar was at 1.3611 Canadian dollars, up from 1.3561 Canadian dollars, and at 8.9226 Swedish krona, higher than the previous day's 8.8813 Swedish krona.

data.title: AI Borrowing Surge and Default Concerns Fail to Deter Bond Investors
data.description: Investors continue to show strong interest in bonds from U.S. companies, states, and cities despite a surge in AI-related borrowing and ongoing concerns about potential defaults. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the resilience of the bond market amid these challenges. The demand for bonds remains robust, driven by investors seeking stable returns in a volatile economic environment. This trend persists even as the borrowing landscape evolves with the increasing influence of artificial intelligence and the associated risks. The bond market's ability to attract investors underscores its role as a critical component of the financial ecosystem, providing essential funding for various entities across the United States.

data.title: Fiserv Launches 24/7 Cash Settlement Platform for Digital Asset Companies
data.description:
Fiserv, a prominent U.S. payments and financial technology provider, has introduced a new cash settlement platform designed for digital asset companies. According to Cointelegraph, this initiative aims to enhance fiat infrastructure for crypto entities and improve liquidity access. The platform, named INDX, was announced on Thursday and offers real-time cash settlement capabilities, operating continuously throughout the year. It enables digital asset firms to transfer U.S. dollars instantly using a single custodial account, potentially transforming how exchanges, trading desks, and other crypto businesses manage their fiat balances.

INDX will be accessible to over 1,100 insured financial institutions within the Fiserv Deposit Network, providing up to $25 million in Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) coverage. This development is significant as many digital asset companies still rely on traditional banking systems that function only during business hours or on onchain token transfers for dollar value movement. By facilitating round-the-clock U.S. dollar settlement within the banking system, INDX offers a functionality akin to blockchain-based settlement while remaining offchain. Fiserv, a global leader in payments and financial services technology, generated over $21 billion in revenue in fiscal 2025. The company has also expanded its presence in digital assets, participating in North Dakota’s state-backed stablecoin initiative by providing payments and settlement infrastructure.

The introduction of INDX exemplifies the ongoing convergence between traditional finance (TradFi) and digital assets. For institutional clients, the platform offers a familiar banking framework while introducing faster, always-on cash management capabilities. This system could position Fiserv ahead of legacy banking partners that still rely on batch-based processing for U.S. dollar transfers. For crypto infrastructure providers, including exchanges, trading desks, stablecoin issuers, and custodians, reliable, real-time dollar liquidity can offer a significant operational advantage. In December, Fiserv completed the acquisition of Stone Castle Cash Management, enhancing its FIUSD stablecoin, launched in June 2025.

Beyond settlement speed, stablecoins are increasingly seen by traditional financial institutions as liquidity infrastructure. Always-on digital dollars can facilitate collateral movement, treasury operations, and cross-border payments with fewer intermediaries and less settlement friction. While INDX is notable for combining traditional bank settlement with continuous-dollar availability tailored to digital-asset companies, other firms have also prioritized real-time settlement. For instance, Sygnum operates a multi-asset network enabling instant settlement across fiat currencies, stablecoins, and other digital assets for institutional clients. Similarly, Fireblocks supports real-time settlement infrastructure for stablecoins and digital asset transfers, aiding institutions in streamlining liquidity management.
